req.title: Cheque deposit by mobile (negative variant)
req.statement: A customer shall deposit a cheque via mobile image; cheques above $5,000 shall require manual verification. Negative paths (insufficient funds, unauthorised, exceeded limits) must be tested.

tests.name: naive_case
tests.executable:
# Pytest Tests for Mobile Cheque Deposit Requirements

```python
"""
Test Suite: Mobile Cheque Deposit via Image
Requirement: Customers shall deposit a cheque via mobile image;
             cheques above $5,000 shall require manual verification.
Negative paths: insufficient funds, unauthorised, exceeded limits.

Author: Quality Engineer
"""

import pytest
from unittest.mock import MagicMock, patch, PropertyMock
from decimal import Decimal
from enum import Enum
from dataclasses import dataclass
from datetime import datetime, timedelta
from typing import Optional


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Domain models (mirroring what the real implementation would expose)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

class DepositStatus(str, Enum):
    PENDING_VERIFICATION = "PENDING_VERIFICATION"
    APPROVED             = "APPROVED"
    REJECTED             = "REJECTED"
    REQUIRES_MANUAL      = "REQUIRES_MANUAL"
    DUPLICATE            = "DUPLICATE"


class RejectionReason(str, Enum):
    INSUFFICIENT_FUNDS       = "INSUFFICIENT_FUNDS"
    UNAUTHORISED             = "UNAUTHORISED"
    DAILY_LIMIT_EXCEEDED     = "DAILY_LIMIT_EXCEEDED"
    MONTHLY_LIMIT_EXCEEDED   = "MONTHLY_LIMIT_EXCEEDED"
    INVALID_IMAGE            = "INVALID_IMAGE"
    DUPLICATE_CHEQUE         = "DUPLICATE_CHEQUE"
    ACCOUNT_SUSPENDED        = "ACCOUNT_SUSPENDED"
    CHEQUE_EXPIRED           = "CHEQUE_EXPIRED"
    AMOUNT_MISMATCH          = "AMOUNT_MISMATCH"
    UNSUPPORTED_CHEQUE_TYPE  = "UNSUPPORTED_CHEQUE_TYPE"


@dataclass
class ChequeImage:
    front_image_bytes: bytes
    back_image_bytes: bytes
    declared_amount: Decimal
    cheque_number: str
    routing_number: str
    account_number: str
    issue_date: datetime


@dataclass
class DepositResult:
    status: DepositStatus
    deposit_id: Optional[str]
    rejection_reason: Optional[RejectionReason]
    requires_manual_review: bool
    message: str
    timestamp: datetime


@dataclass
class CustomerAccount:
    account_id: str
    is_active: bool
    is_verified: bool
    daily_deposit_limit: Decimal
    monthly_deposit_limit: Decimal
    daily_deposited_today: Decimal
    monthly_deposited_this_month: Decimal
    available_balance: Decimal        # issuing-bank balance visible to gateway


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Fake service / SUT (what the real implementation would look like)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

MANUAL_REVIEW_THRESHOLD = Decimal("5000.00")
MAX_CHEQUE_AGE_DAYS     = 180


class InsufficientFundsError(Exception):
    pass

class UnauthorisedError(Exception):
    pass

class DailyLimitExceededError(Exception):
    pass

class MonthlyLimitExceededError(Exception):
    pass

class InvalidImageError(Exception):
    pass

class DuplicateChequeError(Exception):
    pass

class AccountSuspendedError(Exception):
    pass

class ChequeExpiredError(Exception):
    pass


class MobileChequeDepositService:
    """
    Production service stub – the real one would call image-recognition,
    fraud-detection, core-banking, etc.  Here we inject all collaborators
    so they can be mocked in tests.
    """

    def __init__(
        self,
        image_validator,
        fraud_detector,
        core_banking_gateway,
        notification_service,
        audit_logger,
        duplicate_checker,
    ):
        self._image_validator     = image_validator
        self._fraud_detector      = fraud_detector
        self._banking             = core_banking_gateway
        self._notifier            = notification_service
        self._audit               = audit_logger
        self._duplicate_checker   = duplicate_checker

    def deposit(
        self, customer: CustomerAccount, cheque: ChequeImage
    ) -> DepositResult:
        now = datetime.utcnow()

        # ── Guard: account must be active & verified ──────────────────────
        if not customer.is_active:
            raise AccountSuspendedError("Account is suspended.")
        if not customer.is_verified:
            raise UnauthorisedError("Account identity not verified.")

        # ── Guard: image quality ──────────────────────────────────────────
        if not self._image_validator.is_valid(cheque.front_image_bytes,
                                               cheque.back_image_bytes):
            raise InvalidImageError("Cheque image failed quality checks.")

        # ── Guard: cheque age ─────────────────────────────────────────────
        age_days = (now - cheque.issue_date).days
        if age_days > MAX_CHEQUE_AGE_DAYS:
            raise ChequeExpiredError(
                f"Cheque is {age_days} days old (limit {MAX_CHEQUE_AGE_DAYS})."
            )

        # ── Guard: duplicate ──────────────────────────────────────────────
        if self._duplicate_checker.is_duplicate(
            cheque.cheque_number, cheque.routing_number, cheque.account_number
        ):
            raise DuplicateChequeError("Cheque has already been deposited.")

        # ── Limit checks ──────────────────────────────────────────────────
        amount = cheque.declared_amount
        new_daily   = customer.daily_deposited_today   + amount
        new_monthly = customer.monthly_deposited_this_month + amount

        if new_daily > customer.daily_deposit_limit:
            raise DailyLimitExceededError(
                f"Daily limit of {customer.daily_deposit_limit} would be exceeded."
            )
        if new_monthly > customer.monthly_deposit_limit:
            raise MonthlyLimitExceededError(
                f"Monthly limit of {customer.monthly_deposit_limit} would be exceeded."
            )

        # ── Fraud / authorisation check ───────────────────────────────────
        if not self._fraud_detector.approve(customer, cheque):
            raise UnauthorisedError("Transaction declined by fraud engine.")

        # ── Issuing-bank funds check ──────────────────────────────────────
        if customer.available_balance < amount:
            raise InsufficientFundsError(
                "Issuing bank reports insufficient funds."
            )

        # ── Route: manual review vs straight-through ──────────────────────
        requires_manual = amount > MANUAL_REVIEW_THRESHOLD

        if requires_manual:
            deposit_id = self._banking.submit_for_manual_review(customer, cheque)
            self._notifier.notify_manual_review(customer.account_id, deposit_id)
            self._audit.log(customer.account_id, "MANUAL_REVIEW_QUEUED", amount)
            return DepositResult(
                status=DepositStatus.REQUIRES_MANUAL,
                deposit_id=deposit_id,
                rejection_reason=None,
                requires_manual_review=True,
                message="Deposit queued for manual verification.",
                timestamp=now,
            )
        else:
            deposit_id = self._banking.process_deposit(customer, cheque)
            self._notifier.notify_deposit_received(customer.account_id, deposit_id)
            self._audit.log(customer.account_id, "DEPOSIT_PROCESSED", amount)
            return DepositResult(
                status=DepositStatus.APPROVED,
                deposit_id=deposit_id,
                rejection_reason=None,
                requires_manual_review=False,
                message="Deposit accepted.",
                timestamp=now,
            )


# ===========================================================================
# Test Fixtures
# ===========================================================================

@pytest.fixture
def mock_image_validator():
    v = MagicMock()
    v.is_valid.return_value = True          # valid by default
    return v


@pytest.fixture
def mock_fraud_detector():
    fd = MagicMock()
    fd.approve.return_value = True          # approve by default
    return fd


@pytest.fixture
def mock_banking():
    b = MagicMock()
    b.process_deposit.return_value        = "DEP-001"
    b.submit_for_manual_review.return_value = "DEP-MANUAL-001"
    return b


@pytest.fixture
def mock_notifier():
    return MagicMock()


@pytest.fixture
def mock_audit():
    return MagicMock()


@pytest.fixture
def mock_duplicate_checker():
    dc = MagicMock()
    dc.is_duplicate.return_value = False    # unique by default
    return dc


@pytest.fixture
def service(
    mock_image_validator,
    mock_fraud_detector,
    mock_banking,
    mock_notifier,
    mock_audit,
    mock_duplicate_checker,
):
    return MobileChequeDepositService(
        image_validator   = mock_image_validator,
        fraud_detector    = mock_fraud_detector,
        core_banking_gateway = mock_banking,
        notification_service = mock_notifier,
        audit_logger      = mock_audit,
        duplicate_checker = mock_duplicate_checker,
    )


def make_account(**overrides) -> CustomerAccount:
    """Factory for a healthy, default customer account."""
    defaults = dict(
        account_id                  = "ACC-123",
        is_active                   = True,
        is_verified                 = True,
        daily_deposit_limit         = Decimal("10000.00"),
        monthly_deposit_limit       = Decimal("50000.00"),
        daily_deposited_today       = Decimal("0.00"),
        monthly_deposited_this_month= Decimal("0.00"),
        available_balance           = Decimal("99999.99"),
    )
    defaults.update(overrides)
    return CustomerAccount(**defaults)


def make_cheque(**overrides) -> ChequeImage:
    """Factory for a valid default cheque."""
    defaults = dict(
        front_image_bytes = b"FRONT_IMAGE_DATA",
        back_image_bytes  = b"BACK_IMAGE_DATA",
        declared_amount   = Decimal("500.00"),
        cheque_number     = "CHQ-0001",
        routing_number    = "021000021",
        account_number    = "9876543210",
        issue_date        = datetime.utcnow() - timedelta(days=1),
    )
    defaults.update(overrides)
    return ChequeImage(**defaults)


# ===========================================================================
# TEST CLASSES
# ===========================================================================

class TestMobileChequeDepositHappyPath:
    """REQ: Customer shall deposit a cheque via mobile image."""

    def test_deposit_below_threshold_returns_approved_status(
        self, service, mock_banking
    ):
        account = make_account()
        cheque  = make_cheque(declared_amount=Decimal("500.00"))

        result = service.deposit(account, cheque)

        assert result.status == DepositStatus.APPROVED

    def test_deposit_below_threshold_is_not_flagged_for_manual_review(
        self, service
    ):
        account = make_account()
        cheque  = make_cheque(declared_amount=Decimal("4999.99"))

        result = service.deposit(account, cheque)

        assert result.requires_manual_review is False

    def test_deposit_returns_a_deposit_id(self, service, mock_banking):
        account = make_account()
        cheque  = make_cheque(declared_amount=Decimal("100.00"))
        mock_banking.process_deposit.return_value = "DEP-XYZ"

        result = service.deposit(account, cheque)

        assert result.deposit_id == "DEP-XYZ"

    def test_deposit_calls_core_banking_process_once(
        self, service, mock_banking
    ):
        account = make_account()
        cheque  = make_cheque(declared_amount=Decimal("200.00"))

        service.deposit(account, cheque)

        mock_banking.process_deposit.assert_called_once_with(account, cheque)

    def test_deposit_triggers_notification_to_customer(
        self, service, mock_notifier, mock_banking
    ):
        account = make_account(account_id="ACC-456")
        cheque  = make_cheque(declared_amount=Decimal("300.00"))
        mock_banking.process_deposit.return_value = "DEP-789"

        service.deposit(account, cheque)

        mock_notifier.notify_deposit_received.assert_called_once_with(
            "ACC-456", "DEP-789"
        )

    def test_deposit_writes_to_audit_log(self, service, mock_audit):
        account = make_account(account_id="ACC-AUDIT")
        amount  = Decimal("250.00")
        cheque  = make_cheque(declared_amount=amount)

        service.deposit(account, cheque)

        mock_audit.log.assert_called_once_with(
            "ACC-AUDIT", "DEPOSIT_PROCESSED", amount
        )

    def test_deposit_result_has_a_timestamp(self, service):
        before  = datetime.utcnow()
        result  = service.deposit(make_account(), make_cheque())
        after   = datetime.utcnow()

        assert before <= result.timestamp <= after

    @pytest.mark.parametrize("amount", [
        Decimal("0.01"),
        Decimal("1.00"),
        Decimal("999.99"),
        Decimal("2500.00"),
        Decimal("4999.99"),
    ])
    def test_various_amounts_below_threshold_all_auto_approved(
        self, service, amount
    ):
        account = make_account()
        cheque  = make_cheque(declared_amount=amount)

        result = service.deposit(account, cheque)

        assert result.status == DepositStatus.APPROVED
        assert result.requires_manual_review is False


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

class TestManualVerificationThreshold:
    """REQ: Cheques above $5,000 shall require manual verification."""

    def test_cheque_above_5000_requires_manual_review(self, service):
        account = make_account()
        cheque  = make_cheque(declared_amount=Decimal("5000.01"))

        result = service.deposit(account, cheque)

        assert result.requires_manual_review is True

    def test_cheque_above_5000_returns_requires_manual_status(self, service):
        account = make_account()
        cheque  = make_cheque(declared_amount=Decimal("6000.00"))

        result = service.deposit(account, cheque)

        assert result.status == DepositStatus.REQUIRES_MANUAL

    def test_cheque_exactly_5000_does_not_require_manual_review(
        self, service
    ):
        """Boundary: $5,000.00 is NOT above the threshold."""
        account = make_account()
        cheque  = make_cheque(declared_amount=Decimal("5000.00"))

        result = service.deposit(account, cheque)

        assert result.requires_manual_review is False
        assert result.status == DepositStatus.APPROVED

    def test_cheque_at_5000_01_requires_manual_review(self, service):
        """Boundary: one cent above threshold triggers manual path."""
        account = make_account()
        cheque  = make_cheque(declared_amount=Decimal("5000.01"))

        result = service.deposit(account, cheque)

        assert result.requires_manual_review is True

    def test_manual_review_calls_submit_for_manual_review_not_process(
        self, service, mock_banking
    ):
        account = make_account()
        cheque  = make_cheque(declared_amount=Decimal("7500.00"))

        service.deposit(account, cheque)

        mock_banking.submit_for_manual_review.assert_called_once_with(
            account, cheque
        )
        mock_banking.process_deposit.assert_not_called()

    def test_manual_review_notifies_customer_correctly(
        self, service, mock_notifier, mock_banking
    ):
        account = make_account(account_id="ACC-MANUAL")
        cheque  = make_cheque(declared_amount=Decimal("8000.00"))
        mock_banking.submit_for_manual_review.return_value = "DEP-MANUAL-007"

        service.deposit(account, cheque)

        mock_notifier.notify_manual_review.assert_called_once_with(
            "ACC-MANUAL", "DEP-MANUAL-007"
        )
        mock_notifier.notify_deposit_received.assert_not_called()

    def test_manual_review_audit_log_written_correctly(
        self, service, mock_audit
    ):
        account = make_account(account_id="ACC-AUDIT-M")
        amount  = Decimal("9999.99")
        cheque  = make_cheque(declared_amount=amount)

        service.deposit(account, cheque)

        mock_audit.log.assert_called_once_with(
            "ACC-AUDIT-M", "MANUAL_REVIEW_QUEUED", amount
        )

    def test_manual_review_result_contains_deposit_id(
        self, service, mock_banking
    ):
        account = make_account()
        cheque  = make_cheque(declared_amount=Decimal("12000.00"))
        mock_banking.submit_for_manual_review.return_value = "DEP-MANUAL-XYZ"

        result = service.deposit(account, cheque)

        assert result.deposit_id == "DEP-MANUAL-XYZ"

    @pytest.mark.parametrize("amount", [
        Decimal("5000.01"),
        Decimal("10000.00"),
        Decimal("50000.00"),
        Decimal("100000.00"),
    ])
    def test_all_amounts_above_threshold_require_manual_review(
        self, service, amount
    ):
        account = make_account(
            daily_deposit_limit   = Decimal("999999.00"),
            monthly_deposit_limit = Decimal("9999999.00"),
        )
        cheque = make_cheque(declared_amount=amount)

        result = service.deposit(account, cheque)

        assert result.requires_manual_review is True
        assert result.status == DepositStatus.REQUIRES_MANUAL


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

class TestInsufficientFunds:
    """Negative path: issuing bank reports insufficient funds."""

    def test_raises_insufficient_funds_when_balance_too_low(self, service):
        account = make_account(available_balance=Decimal("100.00"))
        cheque  = make_cheque(declared_amount=Decimal("500.00"))

        with pytest.raises(InsufficientFundsError):
            service.deposit(account, cheque)

    def test_zero_balance_raises_insufficient_funds(self, service):
        account = make_account(available_balance=Decimal("0.00"))
        cheque  = make_cheque(declared_amount=Decimal("0.01"))

        with pytest.raises(InsufficientFundsError):
            service.deposit(account, cheque)

    def test_insufficient_funds_does_not_call_core_banking(
        self, service, mock_banking
    ):
        account = make_account(available_balance=Decimal("50.00"))
        cheque  = make_cheque(declared_amount=Decimal("200.00"))

        with pytest.raises(InsufficientFundsError):
            service.deposit(account, cheque)

        mock_banking.process_deposit.assert_not_called()
        mock_banking.submit_for_manual_review.assert_not_called()

    def test_insufficient_funds_does_not_notify_customer(
        self, service, mock_notifier
    ):
        account = make_account(available_balance=Decimal("10.00"))
        cheque  = make_cheque(declared_amount=Decimal("100.00"))

        with pytest.raises(InsufficientFundsError):
            service.deposit(account, cheque)

        mock_notifier.notify_deposit_received.assert_not_called()
        mock_notifier.notify_manual_review.assert_not_called()

    def test_exact_balance_equal_to_amount_does_not_raise(self, service):
        """Boundary: balance == amount should succeed."""
        account = make_account(available_balance=Decimal("500.00"))
        cheque  = make_cheque(declared_amount=Decimal("500.00"))

        result = service.deposit(account, cheque)

        assert result.status == DepositStatus.APPROVED

    def test_one_cent_below_required_balance_raises(self, service):
        account = make_account(available_balance=Decimal("499.99"))
        cheque  = make_cheque(declared_amount=Decimal("500.00"))

        with pytest.raises(InsufficientFundsError):
            service.deposit(account, cheque)

    def test_insufficient_funds_error_message_is_descriptive(self, service):
        account = make_account(available_balance=Decimal("1.00"))
        cheque  = make_cheque(declared_amount=Decimal("1000.00"))

        with pytest.raises(InsufficientFundsError, match="insufficient funds"):
            service.deposit(account, cheque)


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

class TestUnauthorised:
    """Negative path: unauthorised access / identity / fraud decline."""

    def test_unverified_account_raises_unauthorised(self, service):
        account = make_account(is_verified=False)
        cheque  = make_cheque()

        with pytest.raises(UnauthorisedError):
            service.deposit(account, cheque)

    def test_fraud_engine_decline_raises_unauthorised(
        self, service, mock_fraud_detector
    ):
        mock_fraud_detector.approve.return_value = False
        account = make_account()
        cheque  = make_cheque()

        with pytest.raises(UnauthorisedError):
            service.deposit(account, cheque)

    def test_suspended_account_raises_account_suspended_error(
        self, service
    ):
        account = make_account(is_active=False)
        cheque  = make_cheque()

        with pytest.raises(AccountSuspendedError):
            service.deposit(account, cheque)

    def test_unverified_account_does_not_reach_fraud_check(
        self, service, mock_fraud_detector
    ):
        account = make_account(is_verified=False)
        cheque  = make_cheque()

        with pytest.raises(UnauthorisedError):
            service.deposit(account, cheque)

        mock_fraud_detector.approve.assert_not_called()

    def test_fraud_declined_does_not_call_core_banking(
        self, service, mock_fraud_detector, mock_banking
    ):
        mock_fraud_detector.approve.return_value = False
        account = make_account()
        cheque  = make_cheque()

        with pytest.raises(UnauthorisedError):
            service.deposit(account, cheque)

        mock_banking.process_deposit.assert_not_called()
        mock_banking.submit_for_manual_review.assert_not_called()

    def test_suspended_account_does_not_validate_image(
        self, service, mock_image_validator
    ):
        account = make_account(is_active=False)
        cheque  = make_cheque()

        with pytest.raises(AccountSuspendedError):
            service.deposit(account, cheque)

        mock_image_validator.is_valid.assert_not_called()

    def test_invalid_image_raises_invalid_image_error(
        self, service, mock_image_validator
    ):
        mock_image_validator.is_valid.return_value = False
        account = make_account()
        cheque  = make_cheque(
            front_image_bytes=b"BLURRY",
            back_image_bytes=b"BLURRY",
        )

        with pytest.raises(InvalidImageError):
            service.deposit(account, cheque)

    def test_invalid_image_does_not_call_fraud_detector(
        self, service, mock_image_validator, mock_fraud_detector
    ):
        mock_image_validator.is_valid.return_value = False
        account = make_account()
        cheque  = make_cheque()

        with pytest.raises(InvalidImageError):
            service.deposit(account, cheque)

        mock_fraud_detector.approve.assert_not_called()


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

class TestLimitsExceeded:
    """Negative path: daily and monthly deposit limits."""

    def test_daily_limit_exceeded_raises_error(self, service):
        account = make_account(
            daily_deposit_limit   = Decimal("1000.00"),
            daily_deposited_today = Decimal("900.00"),
        )
        cheque = make_cheque(declared_amount=Decimal("200.00"))  # 900+200>1000

        with pytest.raises(DailyLimitExceededError):
            service.deposit(account, cheque)

    def test_monthly_limit_exceeded_raises_error(self, service):
        account = make_account(
            monthly_deposit_limit          = Decimal("5000.00"),
            monthly_deposited_this_month   = Decimal("4900.00"),
        )
        cheque = make_cheque(declared_amount=Decimal("200.00"))  # 4900+200>5000

        with pytest.raises(MonthlyLimitExceededError):
            service.deposit(account, cheque)

    def test_daily_limit_exactly_at_limit_does_not_raise(self, service):
        """Boundary: cumulative total == limit is allowed."""
        account = make_account(
            daily_deposit_limit   = Decimal("1000.00"),
            daily_deposited_today = Decimal("500.00"),
        )
        cheque = make_cheque(declared_amount=Decimal("500.00"))  # 500+500==1000

        result = service.deposit(account, cheque)

        assert result.status == DepositStatus.APPROVED

    def test_one_cent_over_daily_limit_raises(self, service):
        account = make_account(
            daily_deposit_limit   = Decimal("1000.00"),
            daily_deposited_today = Decimal("999.99"),
        )
        cheque = make_cheque(declared_amount=Decimal("0.02"))  # 999.99+0.02>1000

        with pytest.raises(DailyLimitExceededError):
            service.deposit(account, cheque)

    def test_daily_limit_exceeded_does_not_call_core_banking(
        self, service, mock_banking
    ):
        account = make_account(
            daily_deposit_limit   = Decimal("1000.00"),
            daily_deposited_today = Decimal("999.00"),
        )
        cheque = make_cheque(declared_amount=Decimal("500.00"))

        with pytest.raises(DailyLimitExceededError):
            service.deposit(account, cheque)

        mock_banking.process_deposit.assert_not_called()

    def test_monthly_limit_exceeded_does_not_notify_customer(
        self, service, mock_notifier
    ):
        account = make_account(
            monthly_deposit_limit        = Decimal("2000.00"),
            monthly_deposited_this_month = Decimal("1900.00"),
        )
        cheque = make_cheque(declared_amount=Decimal("500.00"))

        with pytest.raises(MonthlyLimitExceededError):
            service.deposit(account, cheque)

        mock_notifier.notify_deposit_received.assert_not_called()

    def test_daily_limit_exceeded_error_message_is_descriptive(
        self, service
    ):
        account = make_account(
            daily_deposit_limit   = Decimal("1000.00"),
            daily_deposited_today = Decimal("900.00"),
        )
        cheque = make_cheque(declared_amount=Decimal("200.00"))

        with pytest.raises(DailyLimitExceededError, match="Daily limit"):
            service.deposit(account, cheque)

    def test_zero_remaining_daily_allowance_raises(self, service):
        account = make_account(
            daily_deposit_limit   = Decimal("500.00"),
            daily_deposited_today = Decimal("500.00"),
        )
        cheque = make_cheque(declared_amount=Decimal("0.01"))

        with pytest.raises(DailyLimitExceededError):
            service.deposit(account, cheque)

    @pytest.mark.parametrize("deposited,limit,amount", [
        (Decimal("9000.00"),  Decimal("10000.00"), Decimal("1001.00")),
        (Decimal("4999.99"),  Decimal("5000.00"),  Decimal("0.02")),
        (Decimal("0.00"),     Decimal("100.00"),   Decimal("100.01")),
    ])
    def test_parameterised_daily_limit_breaches(
        self, service, deposited, limit, amount
    ):
        account = make_account(
            daily_deposit_limit   = limit,
            daily_deposited_today = deposited,
        )
        cheque = make_cheque(declared_amount=amount)

        with pytest.raises(DailyLimitExceededError):
            service.deposit(account, cheque)


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

class TestDuplicateCheque:
    """Negative path: attempt to deposit an already-processed cheque."""

    def test_duplicate_cheque_raises_error(
        self, service, mock_duplicate_checker
    ):
        mock_duplicate_checker.is_duplicate.return_value = True
        account = make_account()
        cheque  = make_cheque()

        with pytest.raises(DuplicateChequeError):
            service.deposit(account, cheque)

    def test_duplicate_cheque_does_not_call_core_banking(
        self, service, mock_duplicate_checker, mock_banking
    ):
        mock_duplicate_checker.is_duplicate.return_value = True
        account = make_account()
        cheque  = make_cheque()

        with pytest.raises(DuplicateChequeError):
            service.deposit(account, cheque)

        mock_banking.process_deposit.assert_not_called()
        mock_banking.submit_for_manual_review.assert_not_called()

    def test_duplicate_checker_called_with_correct_identifiers(
        self, service, mock_duplicate_checker
    ):
        account = make_account()
        cheque  = make_cheque(
            cheque_number  = "CHQ-9999",
            routing_number = "021000021",
            account_number = "1234567890",
        )

        service.deposit(account, cheque)

        mock_duplicate

```python
"""
Test Suite: Mobile Cheque Deposit via Image
Requirement: Customers shall deposit a cheque via mobile image;
             cheques above $5,000 shall require manual verification.
Negative paths: insufficient funds, unauthorised, exceeded limits.

Author: Quality Engineer
"""

import pytest
from unittest.mock import MagicMock, patch, PropertyMock
from decimal import Decimal
from enum import Enum
from dataclasses import dataclass
from datetime import datetime, timedelta
from typing import Optional


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Domain models (mirroring what the real implementation would expose)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

class DepositStatus(str, Enum):
    PENDING_VERIFICATION = "PENDING_VERIFICATION"
    APPROVED             = "APPROVED"
    REJECTED             = "REJECTED"
    REQUIRES_MANUAL      = "REQUIRES_MANUAL"
    DUPLICATE            = "DUPLICATE"


class RejectionReason(str, Enum):
    INSUFFICIENT_FUNDS       = "INSUFFICIENT_FUNDS"
    UNAUTHORISED             = "UNAUTHORISED"
    DAILY_LIMIT_EXCEEDED     = "DAILY_LIMIT_EXCEEDED"
    MONTHLY_LIMIT_EXCEEDED   = "MONTHLY_LIMIT_EXCEEDED"
    INVALID_IMAGE            = "INVALID_IMAGE"
    DUPLICATE_CHEQUE         = "DUPLICATE_CHEQUE"
    ACCOUNT_SUSPENDED        = "ACCOUNT_SUSPENDED"
    CHEQUE_EXPIRED           = "CHEQUE_EXPIRED"
    AMOUNT_MISMATCH          = "AMOUNT_MISMATCH"
    UNSUPPORTED_CHEQUE_TYPE  = "UNSUPPORTED_CHEQUE_TYPE"


@dataclass
class ChequeImage:
    front_image_bytes: bytes
    back_image_bytes: bytes
    declared_amount: Decimal
    cheque_number: str
    routing_number: str
    account_number: str
    issue_date: datetime


@dataclass
class DepositResult:
    status: DepositStatus
    deposit_id: Optional[str]
    rejection_reason: Optional[RejectionReason]
    requires_manual_review: bool
    message: str
    timestamp: datetime


@dataclass
class CustomerAccount:
    account_id: str
    is_active: bool
    is_verified: bool
    daily_deposit_limit: Decimal
    monthly_deposit_limit: Decimal
    daily_deposited_today: Decimal
    monthly_deposited_this_month: Decimal
    available_balance: Decimal        # issuing-bank balance visible to gateway


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Fake service / SUT (what the real implementation would look like)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

MANUAL_REVIEW_THRESHOLD = Decimal("5000.00")
MAX_CHEQUE_AGE_DAYS     = 180

    pass

class UnauthorisedError(Exception):
    pass

class DailyLimitExceededError(Exception):
    pass

class MonthlyLimitExceededError(Exception):
    pass

class InvalidImageError(Exception):
    pass

class DuplicateChequeError(Exception):
    pass

class AccountSuspendedError(Exception):
    pass

class ChequeExpiredError(Exception):
    pass


class MobileChequeDepositService:
    """
    Production service stub – the real one would call image-recognition,
    fraud-detection, core-banking, etc.  Here we inject all collaborators
    so they can be mocked in tests.
    """

    def __init__(
        self,
        image_validator,
        fraud_detector,
        core_banking_gateway,
        notification_service,
        audit_logger,
        duplicate_checker,
    ):

        self._fraud_detector      = fraud_detector
        self._banking             = core_banking_gateway
        self._notifier            = notification_service
        self._audit               = audit_logger
        self._duplicate_checker   = duplicate_checker

    def deposit(
        self, customer: CustomerAccount, cheque: ChequeImage

        now = datetime.utcnow()

        # ── Guard: account must be active & verified ──────────────────────
